title: The Astronomy Tower
prose: It happens at the very end of the year, and it happens fast: Death Eaters inside the castle itself, smuggled in through means nobody fully understands until much later, a fight breaking out across the grounds and towers in the dark. By the time the full story circulates, it's almost too much to take in at once — Draco Malfoy, a boy in your own school, had spent the year on a mission for Voldemort himself, and it ended with Professor Snape, of all people, killing Albus Dumbledore atop the Astronomy Tower before fleeing the grounds.

The leaving feast that follows has no house banners, no house colours at all — just the whole school in black, and Professor McGonagall's voice, usually so steady, audibly unsteady as she asks everyone to remember exactly what Dumbledore stood for.
You'd been at Hogwarts long enough by now to think you understood what the castle could weather. Losing its Headmaster teaches you, all at once, that you didn't.

choices.label: It's the worst thing you've lived through at Hogwarts, by a wide margin.
Grief, yes — but also a hardening resolve about what comes next.
